concepts.name: Binary Search Tree
concepts.short_definition: A binary tree where for each node, all values in its left subtree are less than the node's value, and all values in its right subtree are greater.

concepts.name: Node Structure
concepts.short_definition: The fundamental building block of a tree, typically containing a value and pointers to its children.

concepts.name: Root Node
concepts.short_definition: The topmost node in a tree, from which all other nodes descend.

concepts.name: Insertion Logic
concepts.short_definition: The process of adding a new value to a Binary Search Tree while maintaining its ordering property.

concepts.name: Comparison
concepts.short_definition: The act of comparing a new value with the current node's value to decide which subtree to traverse.

concepts.name: Traversal
concepts.short_definition: The process of visiting each node in a tree in a systematic way, often to find the correct insertion point.

concepts.name: Left Subtree
concepts.short_definition: The part of a binary tree rooted at the left child of a node.

concepts.name: Right Subtree
concepts.short_definition: The part of a binary tree rooted at the right child of a node.

concepts.name: Empty Tree
concepts.short_definition: A tree that contains no nodes.

concepts.name: Recursive Insertion
concepts.short_definition: An insertion method that calls itself to insert elements into subtrees.
